The official YouTube channel of Atlantic Records artist Bruno Mars. 11x GRAMMY Award winner and 27x GRAMMY Award nominee Bruno Mars is a celebrated singer, songwriter, producer, and musician with iconic hits like "The Lazy Song", "That's What I Like", "Just The Way You Are", "24K Magic", "Locked Out Of Heaven", and "When I Was Your Man". His legendary body of work also includes blockbuster albums such as Doo-Wops & Hooligans, Unorthodox Jukebox, and 24K Magic, as well as era-defining collaborations like "Uptown Funk" with Mark Ronson, "Finesse" with Cardi B, and "Nothin' On You" with B.o.B. Forever classic, yet supremely innovative, Bruno continues to redefine music, style, and popular culture, pushing the boundaries of pop, R&B, funk, soul, hip-hop, and dance, and remains as influential as ever.

#BrunoMars #AndersonPaak #SilkSonic #LeaveTheDoorOpen

snippet.title: Starship | SN10 | High-Altitude Flight Test
snippet.description:
As early as Wednesday, March 3, the SpaceX team will attempt a high-altitude flight test of Starship serial number 10 (SN10) ‚Äď our third high-altitude suborbital flight test of a Starship prototype from SpaceX‚Äôs site in Cameron County, Texas. Similar to the high-altitude flight tests of Starship SN8 and SN9, SN10 will be powered through ascent by three Raptor engines, each shutting down in sequence prior to the vehicle reaching apogee ‚Äď approximately 10 km in altitude. SN10 will perform a propellant transition to the internal header tanks, which hold landing propellant, before reorienting itself for reentry and a controlled aerodynamic descent.
  
The Starship prototype will descend under active aerodynamic control, accomplished by independent movement of two forward and two aft flaps on the vehicle. All four flaps are actuated by an onboard flight computer to control Starship‚Äôs attitude during flight and enable precise landing at the intended location. SN10‚Äôs Raptor engines will then reignite as the vehicle attempts a landing flip maneuver immediately before touching down on the landing pad adjacent to the launch mount. 

A controlled aerodynamic descent with body flaps and vertical landing capability, combined with in-space refilling, are critical to landing Starship at destinations across the solar system where prepared surfaces or runways do not exist, and returning to Earth. This capability will enable a fully reusable transportation system designed to carry both crew and cargo on long-duration, interplanetary flights and help humanity return to the Moon, and travel to Mars and beyond.  

Given the dynamic schedule of development testing, stay tuned to our social media channels for updates as we move toward SpaceX‚Äôs third high-altitude flight test of Starship!

snippet.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home - Official Teaser (2021) Tom Holland, Zendaya, Jacob Batalon
snippet.description:
Tom Holland, Zendaya and Jacob Batalon "accidentally" reveal the title for Marvel's Spider-Man 3, directed by Jon Watts. The movie's title is Spider-Man: No Way Home, and it will be released in theaters Christmas 2021.

#IGN #SpiderMan #Marvel

snippet.title: #ABtalks with Nora Fatehi - ŔÖōĻ ŔÜŔąōĪō© ŔĀō™ō≠Ŕä | Chapter 55
snippet.description:
#ABtalks is a YouTube interview show hosted by Anas Bukhash that shines the spotlight on the raw side of celebrities, influencers, and achievers as human beings. Each guest is known for being an inspiration and for being influential as experts in their respective fields. 

Nora Fatehi is a dancer, film actress, and singer who is well known in Bollywood for movies such as Roar: Tigers of the Sundarbans and Satyamev Jayate,¬†in which she was seen in the recreated version of the song Dilbar. On #ABtalks, Nora takes us back to her past, hidden away from the spotlight; from waiting in long queues to prove her talent, traveling across continents to achieve her goals, and ultimately landing blockbuster roles in Bollywood.

Ŕáōįōß ōßŔĄō®ōĪŔÜōßŔÖō¨ ŔáŔą ō≥ŔĄō≥ŔĄō© ŔÖŔÜ ōßŔĄŔÖŔāōßō®ŔĄōßō™ ōßŔĄō≠ŔąōßōĪŔäō© ōßŔĄō™Ŕä ō™ōĻōĪō∂ ōĻŔĄŔČ ōßŔĄŔäŔąō™ŔäŔąō® ŔąŔäŔāōĮŔÖŔáōß ō£ŔÜō≥ ō®ŔąōģōīōĆ ō≠Ŕäōę ŔäŔÉōīŔĀ ōßŔĄō¨ōßŔÜō® ōßŔĄōĶōßōĮŔā 
ŔąōßŔĄō•ŔÜō≥ōßŔÜŔä ŔĄŔĄōĻōĮŔäōĮ ŔÖŔÜ ōßŔĄŔÖōīōßŔáŔäōĪ ŔąōßŔĄōĪŔäōßō∂ŔäŔäŔÜ ŔąōĪŔąōßōĮ ōßŔĄō£ōĻŔÖōßŔĄ ŔąōßŔĄŔÖō§ōęōĪŔäŔÜ ŔąŔäō≥ŔĄō∑ ōßŔĄō∂Ŕąō° ōĻŔĄŔČ ŔāōĶōĶŔáŔÖ ōßŔĄō•ŔÜō≥ōßŔÜŔäō© ōßŔĄŔÖŔĄŔáŔÖō© ŔąōßŔĄŔÖō§ōęōĪō©. 

ŔÜŔąōĪō© ŔĀō™ō≠Ŕä ŔáŔä ōĪōßŔāōĶō© ŔąŔÖŔÖōęŔĄō© ŔąŔÖōļŔÜŔäō© ōßōīō™ŔáōĪō™ ŔĀŔä ō®ŔąŔĄŔäŔąŔąōĮ ŔąōßŔĄōĻōßŔĄŔÖ ō®ōßŔĄō£ŔĀŔĄōßŔÖ ōßŔĄō™Ŕä ŔÖōęŔĄō™ ŔĀŔäŔáōß ŔąōßŔĄō£ōļōßŔÜŔä ōßŔĄō™Ŕä ōļŔÜō™Ŕáōß ŔÖōęŔĄ ōĮŔĄō®ōĪ. ŔĀŔä ŔáōįŔá ōßŔĄō≠ŔĄŔāō©ōĆ ō™ōĻŔäōĮŔÜōß ŔÜŔąōĪō© ō•ŔĄŔČ ŔÖōßō∂ŔäŔáōß ō≠Ŕäōę ō™ōģō®ōĪŔÜōß ōĻŔÜ ōßŔĄōĶōĪōßōĻōßō™ ōßŔĄō™Ŕä Ŕąōßō¨Ŕáō™Ŕáōß ŔąōßŔĄō™ŔÜŔÖōĪ ōßŔĄōįŔä ō™ōĻōĪō∂ō™ ŔĄŔá ŔąōßŔĄōĪŔĀō∂ ōßŔĄōįŔä ō™ŔĄŔāō™Ŕá Ŕāō®ŔĄ ō™ō≠ŔāŔäŔā ō£ŔáōĮōßŔĀŔáōß. ōīōßŔáōĮŔąōß ōßŔĄō≠ŔĄŔāō© ŔĄŔÖōĻōĪŔĀō© ŔāōĶō© ŔÜŔąōĪō© ōßŔĄŔÖŔĄŔáŔÖō©.

Produced by: Bukhash Brothers

snippet.title: Perseverance Rover‚Äôs Descent and Touchdown on Mars (Official NASA Video)
snippet.description:
NASA's Mars 2020 Perseverance mission captured thrilling footage of its rover landing in Mars' Jezero Crater on Feb. 18, 2021.  The real footage in this video was captured by several cameras that are part of the rover's entry, descent, and landing suite. The views include a camera looking down from the spacecraft's descent stage (a kind of rocket-powered jet pack that helps fly the rover to its landing site), a camera on the rover looking up at the descent stage, a camera on the top of the aeroshell (a capsule protecting the rover) looking up at that parachute, and a camera on the bottom of the rover looking down at the Martian surface.
 
The audio embedded in the video comes from the mission control call-outs during entry, descent, and landing. 
 
For more information about Perseverance, visit https://mars.nasa.gov/perseverance
 
Credit: NASA/JPL-Caltech

snippet.title: NASA‚ÄôS Perseverance Rover‚Äôs First 360 View of Mars (Official)
snippet.description:
This video shows the first 360-degree view of the landing site of NASA‚Äôs Perseverance rover on Mars, as captured by the rover‚Äôs color Navigation Cameras, or Navcams. The Navcams are on the remote sensing mast (or ‚Äúhead‚ÄĚ) of the rover.  Perseverance possesses the most cameras of any Mars rover to date, with 19 on the rover. Perseverance landed on Mars‚Äô Jezero Crater on February 18, 2021. These images were obtained on February 20, 2021. 

A key objective for Perseverance‚Äôs mission on Mars is astrobiology, including the search for signs of ancient microbial life. The rover will characterize the planet‚Äôs geology and past climate, pave the way for human exploration of the Red Planet, and be the first mission to collect and cache Martian rock and regolith.


Please note: Not all browsers support viewing 360 videos. YouTube supports their playback on computers using Chrome, Firefox, Internet Explorer, and Opera browsers. For best experience on a mobile device, play this video in the YouTube app.

For more information about Perseverance, visit https://mars.nasa.gov/perseverance


Credit: NASA/JPL-Caltech

 ŔĄŔäōĮōĪ ŔĀŔä ō®ŔĄōĮŔäŔÜ ōßŔÜō™ ōßōģōĪŔÉ ō™ŔĄŔäōĮ ō®ŔĀō©

 ōĪŔÖŔäō≠ ŔāŔĄŔÉ ōßŔÜŔÜōß ō™ōĪŔäŔÜōĮ ōßōĶŔĄōß ŔÖŔÜ Ŕāō®ŔĄ ōßŔĄōĮŔäō≥ōßō™

 ōĪŔąŔā ŔÉōĮōß ōĻōīōßŔÜ ŔĀŔÜŔäōß ŔáŔÜŔÉŔĀŔÜŔÉ 
Ŕą ŔÖō™ōįŔÉōĪōī ō®ōßō™Ŕä ōĻōīōßŔÜ ō®ŔÉŔąō≤ ōßŔĄŔĄōßŔÜōīŔąŔÜ ŔáŔäōļŔĀŔĄŔÉ

ō®ŔäōßŔÉŔĄ ōĻŔäōī ŔāŔąŔĄō™ ōßŔÖōīŔä ŔÖōĻōßŔá ōßŔĄŔÉŔĄōßŔÖ 
ōĻŔāŔĄŔä ō®ŔäŔāŔąŔĄŔČ ōĮŔĄŔąŔāō™Ŕä ŔĀŔÉŔÉ ŔÖŔÜ ōßŔĄŔąōßōĮ ōĮŔá ōßŔÜō≥ōßŔá 
Ŕäōß ōĻŔĀōĪō™ ōĪŔÉō≤ ōĮŔĄŔąŔāō™Ŕä ōĻŔĄŔä ŔÉŔäŔąō®ŔäōĮ ōßŔĄŔÖō¨ōßŔĄ

ŔäŔÉō™ō® ō®ō≥ Ŕą ŔäōīŔąŔĀ ŔāōĮ ōßŔäŔá ŔáŔą ŔáŔąŔáŔąō® Ŕą ōłŔĄŔÖ

ŔĀōß Ŕäō≥Ŕäō® ōßŔĄō™ōĪōßŔÉ Ŕą ŔäōģŔĄōĻ ōĻōīōßŔÜ ŔÖŔäō®ōßŔÜōī ōßŔÜŔá ōßō™ōīō∑ō®

ŔÖōßŔĄŔÜō≠ŔäŔá ōßŔĄō™ōßŔÜŔäŔá ō®ŔäōĻŔÖŔĄ ōīŔäōĪ ŔĄŔĄō™ōĪōßŔÉō™
ōĻōīōßŔÜ ŔĄŔÖōß ŔÜō®ŔāŔä ŔĀŔąŔā ŔäŔāŔąŔĄŔÉ ō∑ŔĄōĻō™ ōßŔĄōīō®ōßō®
ō≥Ŕäōßō≥Ŕá ō≠ŔĄŔąŔá ō®ō≥ ōģō®ōßōęŔá ŔÖŔÜŔÉ Ŕą ōßŔĄōĪōßō®ōĪō≤ ŔáōĪōīŔäŔÜŔáōß

[ ŔĄŔÖōß ō®ō™ŔÜō≤ŔĄ ŔÖōĻ ō≠ōĮ ŔĀ ō™ōĪōßŔÉ ō®ō™ŔÉŔĄŔá Ŕą ōĮŔä ōßŔĄŔÖōīŔÉŔĄŔá 
ōßŔÜō™ Ŕäōß ōĻŔĀōĪō™ ōßŔāŔąŔä ō™ŔÉŔÜŔäŔÉ ŔÖōĶōĪŔä ō≥ŔÖōĻō™Ŕąōß ō®ō¨ōĮ ŔāŔÜō®ŔĄŔá 
ŔĀōß ōßŔÜōß ō® ōßōĻŔÖŔĄŔÉ ŕ§ŔäōĪō≥ ō¨ōßŔÖōĮ ōĻōīōßŔÜ ōßōĶōĮ ŔÖōĻōßŔÉ Ŕą ŔÉōĮōß ]


ŔÖōß ŔáŔą Ŕäōß ōĮŔá ōßŔĄŔĄŔä ŔĀ ŔāŔĄō®ŔÉ Ŕą ōßŔÜō™ ōĮŔĄŔąŔāō™Ŕä ō®ō™ŔáŔĀŔĄō∑ 
ōßŔą ō≥ōßōĻō™Ŕáōß ŔÉŔÜō™ ō®ō™ŔāŔąŔĄ ŔÉōĮōß Ŕą ō®ō≥ ōĻōīōßŔÜ ō™ōĻōĪō∂ 
Ŕą ŔĀ ōßŔĄō≠ōßŔĄō™ŔäŔÜ ōßŔÜō™ ŔÉōĮōß ō®ŔąōīŔäŔÜ ŔĀ ōßō™ōĶōĪŔĀ 
ōīŔÉŔĄŔÉ ōģōĪŔá ŔāōĮōßŔÖ ōßŔĄŔĀōßŔÜō≤ ō®ōĮō£Ŕąōß Ŕäō≠ō≥Ŕąōß ōßŔÜŔÉ ō®ō™ōģōĪŔĀ

Ŕą ōßŔĄōīō®ōßō® Ŕą ōßŔāŔĀŔá ō®ō™ō≥ŔāŔĀ ōßōĶŔĄōß ōĻōßŔĄōļŔĄ ō™ō™ō≥ōßō®Ŕā 
ō®Ŕāō™ŔĄŔÉ ŔÖŔÜ ōļŔäōĪ ŔÖōß ōßō≠ō≥ō≥ ō≠ōĮ Ŕą ōßŔĄō≥ōĪ ŔĀ ōßŔĄŔÉōßō™ŔÖ

ōßŔÖōß ō®ōßŔĄŔÜō≥ō®Ŕá ŔĄŔĄŔĀōßŔÜō≤ ōĻōßōĪŔĀ ōßŔÜŔä ŔāŔáŔąō¨Ŕä

snippet.title: Watch NASA‚Äôs Perseverance Rover Land on Mars!
snippet.description:
Watch an epic journey unfold on Thursday, Feb. 18 as our Perseverance rover lands on Mars. To reach the surface of the Red Planet, the rover has to survive the harrowing final phase known as Entry, Descent, and Landing.
 
Only then can the rover ‚Äď the biggest, heaviest, cleanest, and most sophisticated six-wheeled robot ever launched into space ‚Äď search Jezero Crater for signs of ancient life and collect samples that will eventually be returned to Earth.
 
Tune in to a live video feed of key landing activities and commentary from Mission Control at NASA‚Äôs Jet Propulsion Laboratory.
